title: Add pane-focused keyboard navigation
description: Keyboard selection should start in the Ledger daily usage pane. Tab should cycle the active pane, and arrow/page keys should operate inside that pane without scrolling the document.
close_reason: Implemented Ledger active-pane keyboard navigation: daily usage starts active, Tab cycles panes, arrows/page keys act inside the active pane, and browser checks confirm document scroll remains locked.

title: Restore Ledger panels inside fixed viewport
description: The previous fixed-viewport change made the Ledger surface lose its graph and supporting panels for some viewports. Restore the old Ledger composition while keeping document-level scrolling disabled and arrow-key selection behavior.
close_reason: Restored explicit Ledger panel placement within the fixed viewport and verified all four Ledger panels render at desktop, medium, and narrow widths without document scroll.

title: Phase 7: Migration, backup, and release hardening
description: Add consistent local-database seeding during first Hub deployment, idempotent Collector backfill, verified daily and pre-upgrade backups, download/restore/retention controls, one-release legacy remote deprecation, and the real-tailnet Linux Hub plus Linux/macOS Collector release gate.
acceptance_criteria: Existing history seeds without manual file imports or duplicate backfill; backup retention, verification, restore, restart, and failed-update rollback pass; legacy remote guidance ships; real-tailnet proof meets ten-second freshness, offline replay, privacy, and platform acceptance.

dirtyloop_phase.outcome: History migration, backup and restore, deprecation, restart, rollback, and real-tailnet release proof are complete.
quality_gates: migration, backup, restore, rollback, and real-tailnet gates

backup verification or restore cannot meet durability requirements
real-tailnet topology fails required latency, privacy, restart, or rollback acceptance

acceptance_criteria: Frontend and browser tests cover keyboard and touch navigation, range/breakdown/sort/scale changes, live updates without selection loss, reduced motion, empty/error/stale states, phone reductions, all themes, and WCAG AA contrast.

title: Phase 3: Collector runtime

acceptance_criteria: All five Agent fixtures pass; malformed input and parser upgrades are covered; offline outbox replay is at-least-once and deduplicated; redaction prevents raw content and absolute paths from request payloads; watcher failures fall back to reconciliation.

title: reconcile dependent dirtydash prs

title: Clarify fast-mode accounting for Codex usage
description: Dirtydash currently presents cached-input pricing as fast usage, which can understate or misrepresent Codex subscription drain when users run xhigh sessions mostly in fast mode. Investigate importer and UI labeling so fast-mode/token-limit accounting is distinguished from cache-read cost estimates.
close_reason: Added dashboard copy that separates Codex fast-mode subscription drain from cache-read/API-style cost estimates.

title: Resolve merge conflicts on PR #2
description: PR #2 is dirty against main. Resolve the merge conflicts on lavender/accounting-diff-investigation, validate the repository, document the turn if files change, and push the branch so the PR can merge cleanly.
close_reason: Resolved PR #2 merge conflicts by merging origin/main into lavender/accounting-diff-investigation, preserving both importer fixes in importers.rs, keeping Beads records from both sides, and validating with cargo fmt --check, cargo test -p dirtydash, and dashboard build.

title: Fix Codex token_count accounting
description: DirtyDash undercounts Codex costs because Codex token_count rows expose per-request usage in payload.info.last_token_usage, while the importer recursively extracts that value and then treats it as cumulative. Update the parser to explicitly read last_token_usage as the event increment, use total_token_usage only as a fallback/delta source, and add regression coverage against current Codex JSONL shape.
notes: Validation detail: temp import into /tmp/dirtydash-accounting-fixed.sqlite3 produced Codex 30d base-rate estimate $971.05 and all-time Codex base-rate estimate $1314.55 after the parser and cached-input split fix.
close_reason: Fixed Codex token_count accounting by reading payload.info.last_token_usage as the event increment, splitting cached input from non-cached input for Codex rows, and adding regression coverage. Validation: cargo test -p dirtydash; temp import produced Codex 30d base-rate estimate .05.

title: Price imported Codex model aliases correctly
description: DirtyDash is undercounting estimated spend because imported Codex model identifiers can land in zero-dollar/unpriced model rows even when they correspond to priced OpenAI models. Add model alias handling and validation so aggregated spend reflects those token rows.
close_reason: Fixed imported Codex/pi-agent spend undercounting by parsing nested usage, using source-reported costs, aliasing Codex/OpenAI pricing identifiers, updating stale imported rows on reimport, adding regressions, and updating the dashboard to surface unpriced tokens.

title: Scaffold dirtydash V1 local-first foundation

Follow-up remote import and fixture hardening are tracked separately.

title: Dirtydash refresh, remote sync, harness, layout, and theme loop
description: Create the Beads-canonical dirtyloops stream for the finalized Dirtydash refresh, remote sync, harness, layout, and theme plan. The stream implements phases 1-5 from the plan: refresh foundation, Ledger layout reshape, themes, agentless SSH remote sync, and OpenCode/Hermes harness support. Live watcher/SSE is explicitly future scope and tracked separately. Workflow is orchestrator-callback with a runtime-supplied exact callback target id, callback-wait coordination, worker swarm-first implementation, strict review using thermo-nuclear-code-quality-review, and reviewer-owned CI. Docs live under docs/implementation/refresh-remote-harness-layout-theme.
acceptance_criteria: Loop is runnable from Beads and docs, all five in-scope phases have child issues and phase docs, and future watcher/SSE work remains filed separately instead of widening the stream.

title: Calculate cache savings in dashboard stats
description: Dirtydash currently returns cache_stats.estimated_savings_usd as 0.0 even when cached input tokens are priced below normal input tokens. Compute the estimated savings from usage_events joined to pricing_records so the dashboard can show the effect of cached-token discounts.
close_reason: Implemented cache savings calculation and dashboard display; verified with cargo test -p dirtydash and dashboard build.

title: Add dirtyloops loop upgrade command
description: Add a dirtydash CLI command that can upgrade an existing dirtyloops-generated loop directory to the currently installed dirtyloops runtime templates and schemas without mutating phase docs or Beads state by default.
close_reason: Implemented dirtydash loop upgrade command with check/dry-run modes, docs, tests, and real-template smoke verification.

title: Auto-update session files and outputs
description:
DirtyDash should seamlessly update session files and outputs as local AI tool traces change, instead of requiring a manual scan/import/reload mental model.

What needs to be done:
- Design the live update path: server-side file watcher/incremental import, UI polling/SSE/WebSocket refresh, or a phased combination.
- Reuse existing importer idempotency (`raw_event_hash`) so repeated updates do not duplicate usage events.
- Surface update state in the dashboard: fresh/updating/stale/error, plus source file/output provenance.
- Preserve the local-first/privacy posture; avoid exposing conversation contents unless a separate redacted-preview decision is made.

Existing context:
- README V2 mentions live session tailing and richer Import/Files views.
- The data model already carries `raw_path`, `raw_span`, parser metadata, and `imported_at` on session/usage rows.
- Current React load path fetches dashboard data once on mount and only refetches day sessions when the selected day changes.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance:
- A running DirtyDash dashboard can reflect newly changed/imported session data without a full manual reload.
- Incremental imports/refreshes are idempotent and do not duplicate usage events.
- Session/file/output freshness is visible with provenance and stale/error states.
- Privacy remains metadata-first unless a separate redacted-output-preview decision is approved.
- Tests or smoke checks cover the chosen refresh/import behavior.

title: Add live session updates and keyboard-first command chrome
description:
Capture Kell's idea to make DirtyDash feel live and keyboard-native instead of static/manual.

Scope:
- Seamless automatic updating of session files and outputs so the dashboard follows active AI coding work without manual refresh/reimport friction.
- Replace the current fake/current CLI command header treatment with TUI-native UI controls and keyboard shortcut hints.

Context:
- README already lists live session tailing and richer Import/Files views as future work.
- Current dashboard fetches summary/sources/sessions/pricing/doctor on load, and day sessions on selected-day changes.
- The current header renders a synthetic command string such as `dirtydash <workspace> --sort ... --view ... --graph ...`; Kell wants UI elements that preserve the TUI aesthetic instead.

Alignment needed before implementation:
- Should live updates be implemented first as a server-side file watcher/import loop, a UI polling/refresh loop over already-imported data, or both?
- Should "outputs" mean raw transcript/output previews, metadata-only output file state, or selectable redacted previews?

Add fork-aware Codex delta parsing so remaining Dirtydash/CodexBar reconciliation deltas can be attributed to date windows or cache freshness instead of parser semantics.

title: show gpt-5.5 fast model totals separately

title: Consolidate model slugs and fast mode usage

Align bundled pricing semantics with Cursor's models and pricing table where practical, especially treating cache write as part of input/cache-write pricing rather than an extra GPT-only write surcharge.

title: Clarify cache metrics when writes are not reported

Update the UI copy so users understand Dirtydash reports observed cache reads and only shows write tokens when a source actually reports them.

title: Model Codex Fast and long-context pricing
description:
DirtyDash now parses Codex token_count rows correctly for base-rate accounting, but final totals still diverge from CodexBar/TokScale: current base-rate import was ~$971.05 (last 30d) and ~$1,314.55 (all-time) in a clean reimport, while CodexBar still reports ~$1,241 last-30d and TokScale reports ~$1.6k all-time.

Add support for Codex service-tier and context-window pricing so the remaining gap is explainable and matchable.

What to inspect:
- Source JSONL fields that indicate tiering, e.g. model naming suffixes/prefixes, metadata labels, or dedicated fields that map to "fast" and "priority" processing.
- Long-context markers (input/output token bands, model family, or row-level flags) that should switch to elevated per-token rates.
- How CodexBar decides whether to apply surcharge tiers for each event (per-session vs per-request, and whether cached tokens participate).
- Whether surcharge/long-context pricing is based on model alias, window crossing thresholds, or a fixed uplift for certain rows.
- Whether any post-processing in CodexBar is applied per model family after import.

Required implementation work:
1) Add new pricing metadata keys for Codex fast/priority and long-context rates.
2) Update importer to persist codex tier/context attributes on parsed rows so cost math can be applied deterministically.
3) Extend pricing resolver to apply tiered rates and add regression tests for each branch (fast, priority, long-context, and cached-token behavior).
4) Add reporting that surfaces base-rate vs fast/priority/long-context components separately in aggregate views and per-session breakdowns.
5) Reconcile by running a temp import and document: imported-cost, CodexBar last-30d, all-time baseline, and a delta explanation table.
acceptance_criteria: Acceptance: dirtydash can compute Codex costs with three clearly attributable buckets: base-rate, fast/priority surcharge, and long-context surcharge. Implement deterministic mapping from payload/model hints to service tier and threshold. Add tests for fast vs non-fast, priority, long-context and cached tokens so deltas close to CodexBar/TokScale are explainable. Update docs/labels so users can inspect which rows used each rate.
close_reason: implemented codexbar-style codex pricing modes, local logs_2 priority evidence, priority aggregate splits, shared fast usage bars, pricing-page usage bars, tests, visual checks, and turn documentation. broader codexbar 30-day reconciliation remains tracked by dirtydash-q9g.

title: Reconcile DirtyDash estimated spend with CodexBar 30-day total
description: DirtyDash now prices previously zero-dollar Codex/pi-agent rows by importing nested usage and reported costs, but a clean temp import totals about $456.81 while CodexBar reports about $1204 for the last 30 days. Investigate CodexBar's exact data source, date window, and pricing semantics so DirtyDash can explain or match the remaining gap.
acceptance_criteria: DirtyDash can show a reconciliation view or documented calculation that explains the difference from CodexBar's last-30-days total, including source coverage, date-window boundaries, and pricing assumptions.
close_reason: aligned Dirtydash Codex source coverage, priority pricing, and dashboard token summaries with CodexBar; filed dirtydash-ejj for remaining fork-baseline nuance

title: Harden importers with real-world source fixtures
description: Collect representative Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and pi-agent fixtures from real installations and tighten parsers around edge cases that are not covered by the synthetic V1 scaffold tests.

title: Import usage files from SSH remotes
description: Extend V1 remote sync beyond read-only file discovery so it can pull or stream known JSON/JSONL usage files over SSH/SFTP, import them into SQLite, and preserve remote machine provenance for each usage event.

title: Fix dirtyloops v2 idempotency detection for preserved loop state
description: The dirtyloops migrate-v2 helper scans loop-state.md for legacy tokens after migration. This stream must preserve authored history and the explicit delegated-thread xhigh constraint, so the second required migration run sees orchestrator-callback/reasoning: xhigh and attempts to archive the v2 IMPLEMENT.md over the preserved v1 copy, failing with an archive collision instead of returning already-v2. Fix the helper to distinguish preserved loop-state history and explicit user constraints from active generated v1 policy.
acceptance_criteria: A migrated loop with preserved v1 history or explicit model/effort constraints in loop-state.md returns already-v2 on the second identical helper invocation without changing the v1 archive or active generated files.

title: Future: Live watcher and SSE refresh
description: Future work from the finalized plan, intentionally outside the current dirtyloop stream: local filesystem watcher over configured source roots, debounced metadata-only refresh, dashboard notification by SSE or lightweight polling, and graceful fallback to manual refresh. Do not require remote daemons.
acceptance_criteria: After explicit refresh and remote sync stabilize, design and implement a local watcher plus dashboard notification path that degrades cleanly to manual refresh.

dirtyloop_followup.reason: Plan phase 6 is explicitly future scope after explicit refresh and remote sync are stable

title: Replace command header with TUI shortcut chrome
description:
Replace the current synthetic CLI command header with TUI-native UI elements that communicate controls and state without pretending the user is running a command.

What needs to be done:
- Replace the `dirtydash <workspace> --sort ... --view ... --graph ...` command-line header with compact command-palette/status chrome.
- Prefer keyboard shortcut hints for most controls: workspace numbers, Tab pane/tab cycling, `/` search, `?` help, `s` sort, `v` view, `g` graph, arrow/j/k navigation.
- Keep the terminal-observatory/TUI aesthetic: dense, calm, keyboard-first, aligned, no SaaS-y toolbar gloss.
- Preserve accessibility: real labels/titles, shortcuts ignored while typing, shortcut overlay remains discoverable.

Existing context:
- `dashboard/src/main.tsx` builds `commandText` and renders it in `.command-bar` as the current command context.
- The app already has keyboard navigation, a shortcut overlay, rail shortcut hints, and mode pills for sort/view/chart/row state.
acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance:
- The command bar no longer presents a fake/current CLI command as the primary header.
- Users see compact TUI-style shortcut hints and state controls for the active workspace.
- Existing shortcuts continue to work and remain disabled while typing in inputs.
- The shortcut overlay and accessibility labels/titles remain accurate.
- Browser/dashboard smoke verifies the replacement chrome renders cleanly at desktop and narrow widths.

title: Add live source watcher and dashboard event stream
description: Future refresh path after explicit refresh is stable. Add an optional local filesystem watcher over configured source roots, debounce writes from active session logs, trigger the existing server-side refresh manager, and notify the dashboard over SSE or equivalent so new sessions appear without manual reload. Keep it best-effort and local-only; do not require remote daemons for this feature.
design: Build after POST /api/refresh and single-flight refresh state exist. Prefer notify-based watching plus SSE/status polling from the local Rust server; avoid parsing files in the browser.
acceptance_criteria:
- Watcher can be enabled without blocking dashboard startup
- Changes in known local source roots trigger a debounced metadata-only refresh
- Dashboard receives a lightweight refresh-complete event and updates without losing selection
- File watching failures degrade to manual refresh with a visible status
